task_completion.description: Navigate to the Tor Project Community page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displays the URL https://community.torproject.org/ in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Tor Project Metrics page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displays the URL https://metrics.torproject.org/ in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Tor Project Blog page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displays the URL https://blog.torproject.org/ in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Save the three HTML files in the target directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or terminal window shows the directory /home/ga/Documents/TorResearch/ containing the files 'tor-community.html', 'tor-metrics.html', and 'tor-blog.html'.

task_completion.description: Create the bibliography file with the correct header
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the file 'bibliography.txt' (located in /home/ga/Documents/TorResearch/) with the exact header text 'Research Bibliography - Tor Network Article'.

task_completion.description: Include the three visited URLs and brief descriptions in the bibliography
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'bibliography.txt' file in the text editor contains the three Tor Project URLs (community, metrics, and blog) and each URL is accompanied by a brief one-line description of the page.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical web browser to visit the pages
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a GUI web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) rendering the Tor Project pages, rather than the agent exclusively using terminal commands like curl or wget to fetch the pages.

integrity.description: The HTML files were genuinely saved from the browser
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the browser's 'Save Page As' dialog being used, or the resulting files should have file sizes indicating actual HTML content, rather than being empty files created via the 'touch' command.

integrity.description: The descriptions in the bibliography are genuinely written
integrity.visual_evidence: The one-line descriptions in bibliography.txt should reflect the actual content of the respective Tor pages, rather than being generic placeholder text or exact copy-pastes of the prompt instructions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target directory must be exactly '/home/ga/Documents/TorResearch'. The required files to be saved are 'tor-community.html', 'tor-metrics.html', 'tor-blog.html', and 'bibliography.txt'. The required URLs to visit and document are 'community.torproject.org', 'metrics.torproject.org', and 'blog.torproject.org'.
